summary:
The submandibular gland primarily gets its arterial supply via the facial artery, one of the branches of the external carotid artery. This information aligns consistently among multiple medical literature resources consulted.
The submandibular gland mainly obtains its arterial supply from the facial artery, identified as a direct branch of the external carotid artery; several studies corroborate these details regarding vascular anatomy.
